title: Privacy Policy — DIGI-METER
description: DIGI-METER Privacy Policy — how we collect, use, and protect your personal data in accordance with GDPR.

strings.source: How DIGI-METER collects, uses, and protects your personal data — and how to exercise your rights under GDPR.
strings.value: How DIGI-METER collects, uses, and protects your personal data — and how to exercise your rights under GDPR.

strings.source: The DIGI-METER project is an Erasmus+ Forward-Looking project co-funded by the European Union under Grant Agreement No. 101259860. The data controller responsible for this website and its data processing activities is:
strings.value: The DIGI-METER project is an Erasmus+ Forward-Looking project co-funded by the European Union under Grant Agreement No. 101259860. The data controller responsible for this website and its data processing activities is:

strings.source: Project lead for communication and dissemination
strings.value: Project lead for communication and dissemination

strings.source: Responsibility for learner assessment data on the DIGI-METER platform (once launched) will be described in a separate, detailed privacy notice published together with the platform.
strings.value: Responsibility for learner assessment data on the DIGI-METER platform (once launched) will be described in a separate, detailed privacy notice published together with the platform.

strings.value: All platform data will be processed under a separate, detailed privacy notice presented at registration time.

strings.source: only for storing your accessibility preferences (contrast mode, font size) and form draft state. These values never leave your browser. See our

strings.source: We take appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. Form submissions are sent over an encrypted connection (HTTPS) and delivered by email to the project team. Access to contact form data is restricted to the project communication lead (Outside Media & Knowledge) and relevant consortium partners on a need-to-know basis.
strings.value: We take appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. Form submissions are sent over an encrypted connection (HTTPS) and delivered by email to the project team. Access to contact form data is restricted to the project communication lead (Outside Media & Knowledge) and relevant consortium partners on a need-to-know basis.

strings.source: This public website is not directed at children under 16 and does not knowingly collect data from them. The DIGI-METER platform (when launched) will process learner data from secondary school students under appropriate institutional data processing agreements and parental/guardian consent mechanisms as required by national law.
strings.value: This public website is not directed at children under 16 and does not knowingly collect data from them. The DIGI-METER platform (when launched) will process learner data from secondary school students under appropriate institutional data processing agreements and parental/guardian consent mechanisms as required by national law.

strings.source: We may update this Privacy Policy as the project progresses, particularly when the assessment platform launches. Material changes will be announced on this website. The version number and update date at the top of this page will reflect the latest revision.
strings.value: We may update this Privacy Policy as the project progresses, particularly when the assessment platform launches. Material changes will be announced on this website. The version number and update date at the top of this page will reflect the latest revision.
